message: bd-lff4p.10.4 land websocket PTY bridge and smoke test

@@ -1323,7 +1323,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm VT/ANSI support matrix + conformance fixtures","description":"Goal
- Define exactly what FrankenTerm will implement (and what it will intentionally NOT implement) for VT/ANSI output streams.

Deliverables
- Support matrix for: C0/C1 controls, CSI sequences, SGR, OSC (incl OSC 8), DEC private modes, cursor modes, scrolling, insert/delete, tabs.
- A conformance fixture corpus (byte streams + expected grid snapshots) with deterministic replay.
- Clear "compat" boundaries: which behaviors must match de-facto terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Parser work can be driven directly from this matrix and fixtures.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449662450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449643043Z","close_reason":"Wrote comprehensive VT/ANSI support matrix at docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md covering: C0/C1 controls, CSI (cursor, erase, insert/delete, scroll, modes), SGR (attributes, 16/256/RGB colors), OSC (title, hyperlinks, clipboard), DEC private modes, Kitty keyboard protocol, character sets. Includes 15 sections, explicit priority levels (Must/Should/Won't), parser state machine spec, conformance fixture format with 6 initial fixtures, and CI integration plan.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","title":"Bench: parser throughput + patch apply cost (core engine)","description":"Goal
- Build repeatable benchmarks for the core engine so optimization work is rigorous and gated.

Bench Corpus
- Maintain a realistic byte-stream corpus (build logs, dense SGR, markdown-ish output, unicode-heavy lines).
- Every benchmark run records corpus id/hash + git_sha so results are comparable.

Metrics
- Parser throughput (MB/s) for each corpus stream.
- Patch generation + patch apply cost under typical workloads.
- Memory footprint per 1k lines scrollback and under resize storms.

Outputs
- Machine-readable results (JSON) suitable for CI regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Variance is characterized and documented; thresholds are set for meaningful regressions (p95 or distributional tests).
","notes":"2026-02-09 RainyMill follow-up: added scrollback_memory and resize_storm benchmark groups to parser_patch_bench, emitted machine-readable JSON events for memory/resize metrics, expanded bench_budget parser mappings and budgets for parser_throughput_large/full_pipeline/patch_diff_apply/resize_storm/scrollback_memory, and validated with cargo check/clippy/fmt plus ./scripts/bench_budget.sh --quick --json.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:06:02.018299494Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:57:59.666788056Z","close_reason":"Bench landed in 4cabfd1d; bd-lff4p.1.6 is closed; verified with cargo check -p frankenterm-core --benches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.11","title":"Fuzz: frankenterm-core parser and grid mutation entrypoints","description":"Goal
- Add fuzz targets to harden the parser and mutation logic.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fuzz harnesses run locally and in CI (time-bounded) with crash artifacts captured.
- Any found crashes become regression fixtures in the conformance corpus.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-core (API skeleton + workspace wiring)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-core` with the minimal public API surface and internal module layout.

Notes
- Keep dependencies minimal.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core` passes (native).
- Basic types compile: Grid, Cell, Parser, Patch.
","notes":"Implemented crate skeleton in crates/frankenterm-core (Cell/SgrAttrs, Cursor+SavedCursor, Grid ops, Modes flags, Parser skeleton emitting Actions, Patch + Scrollback skeleton). Wired into workspace Cargo.toml. Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core, cargo test -p frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512397161Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512374489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262569874Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262549566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","title":"Implement: scrollback + resize/reflow policy","description":"Goal
- Support realistic scrollback and correct behavior under resize.

Requirements
- Scrollback buffer with configurable capacity.
- Clear reflow policy: what happens to wrapped lines on resize.
- Resize invariants: cursor position, viewport, selection (if any), and mode semantics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic replay fixtures for resize storms.
- Bench coverage for resize cost and memory.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:09.224829755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:09.224807123Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1336,7 +1336,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm websocket protocol + threat model","description":"Goal
- Define the browser <-> PTY transport protocol and a production-safe threat model.

Must Cover
- Message types
  - input bytes / semantic events
  - resize
  - output chunks
  - terminal replies (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - clipboard (optional)
  - keepalive
- Versioning + schema
  - protocol version negotiation
  - JSON Schema / binary schema (explicit)
- Backpressure + flow control (prevent OOM)
  - bounded queues
  - coalescing policies (resize storms, mouse move floods)
  - fairness: interactive input must not be starved by output
- Security model
  - origin restrictions, auth, rate limits
  - allowed commands (if any), sandboxing
  - no implicit file access
  - explicit threat model + mitigations
- Capability identity
  - TERM/capability profile story for remote PTY sessions (ties to terminfo bead)
- Logging
  - every session emits JSONL with run_id/session_id, git_sha/build_id, sizes, wire counters, latency histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol is versioned and has a schema.
- E2E scripts can generate deterministic transcripts for golden checks.
- Threat model includes an explicit loss matrix (false allow vs false block) and a documented decision policy.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltDog","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:49.528985806Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:49.528951862Z","close_reason":"Protocol spec complete: 761-line document covering message types, flow control, security, logging","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","title":"Implement: browser input encoder (DOM events -> terminal byte stream)","description":"Goal
- Encode browser keyboard/mouse events into a VT-compatible input byte stream suitable for a remote PTY.

Scope
- Key encoding: arrows, function keys, modifiers, paste, and common shortcuts.
- Mouse reporting sequences when enabled (SGR mouse).
- Respect feature toggles (bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard where applicable).

Testing
- Unit tests for key/mouse mapping tables.
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck mods).
- JSONL logs for E2E sessions include encoded byte lengths and event counts.
","notes":"Implemented VT-compatible browser input encoder in frankenterm-web. Added VtInputEncoderFeatures toggles (sgr_mouse, bracketed_paste, focus_events, kitty_keyboard) and encode_vt_input_event() pipeline covering key/mouse/wheel/focus/composition-commit events. Added legacy xterm key encoding (arrows/function keys/modifiers/common ctrl shortcuts), Kitty keyboard encoding with press/repeat/release kinds, SGR mouse + wheel encoding, bracketed paste helper for committed composition text, and feature-gated focus/mouse behavior. Wired wasm ingestion to queue encoded VT byte chunks and exposed drainEncodedInputBytes() for remote PTY forwarding; init options now parse encoder toggles. Added encoder unit tests for key mappings, modifier forms, mouse/focus gating, composition commit paste wrapping, and kitty release encoding. Validation: cargo test -p frankenterm-web (pass, including new tests), cargo check -p frankenterm-web --all-targets (pass), cargo clippy -p frankenterm-web --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass), cargo check --all-targets (workspace pass), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (workspace pass), cargo fmt --check (workspace pass). Note: cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown currently fails in pre-existing renderer API drift (wgpu surface/request_device signatures), outside this bead scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightDune","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:30:58.206597545Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:30:58.206577637Z","close_reason":"Completed: VT input encoder + tests + wasm byte-drain wiring","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","title":"Implement: terminal reply engine (DSR/DA/DEC queries)","description":"Goal
- Support the bidirectional terminal contract by answering application queries (e.g. cursor position, device attributes).

Scope
- Identify and implement the minimal required set of queries for common CLI apps.
- Replies must be deterministic and capability-profiled.

Testing
- Conformance fixtures: query -> expected reply bytes.
- Differential tests against reference terminals where possible.
","notes":"2026-02-09 CoralRidge: claiming from stale state after triage/agent coordination; implementing DSR/DA/DEC reply engine coverage + tests in frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadePrairie","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:12:19.429859791Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:12:19.429841106Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented deterministic DSR/DA/DEC reply engine in frankenterm-core with tests and full workspace gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.324667191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.4","title":"Implement: PTY bridge server (Rust) with websocket transport","description":"Goal
- Implement a Rust service that spawns a PTY and bridges I/O over websockets to FrankenTerm.WASM.

Scope
- Use portable-pty (already in tree) or a better alternative if justified.
- Enforce security constraints from the threat model.
- Expose structured telemetry: bytes in/out, latency, resize counts, exit status.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can run locally and drive interactive shells.
- Produces JSONL logs conforming to the shared schema.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.5","title":"E2E: scripted remote terminal sessions + golden transcripts","description":"Goal
- Add end-to-end scripts that connect the browser FrankenTerm to the PTY bridge and run deterministic scripted sessions.

Requirements
- Scripts must emit detailed JSONL (run_id, git_sha, sizes, wire counters, checksums).
- Golden transcripts are checksum-gated; failures produce actionable diffs.

Acceptance Criteria
- At least 5 high-signal scripts: resize storm, paste, unicode, OSC8 links, and long scrollback.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","title":"Perf: keystroke-to-photon latency budget + measurement harness","description":"Goal
- Define and enforce latency budgets for interactive remote sessions.

Metrics
- Keystroke -> rendered frame (p50/p95/p99).
- Round-trip websocket latency and jitter.
- Output throughput under load.

Acceptance Criteria
- Harness outputs machine-readable JSON and is gated against regressions.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","title":"Spec: TERM/capability profile + terminfo for FrankenTerm remote sessions","description":"Goal
- Ensure remote PTY apps behave correctly by defining FrankenTerm's terminal identity and capabilities.

Scope
- Decide what TERM will be set to in the PTY (e.g. `frankenterm`, `xterm-256color`, or a compatibility alias).
- If `TERM=frankenterm`, define and ship a terminfo entry (and a versioning story).
- Map terminfo capabilities to the VT/ANSI support matrix and reply engine behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- A pinned capability profile exists (colors, mouse, paste, focus, kitty, hyperlinks) with explicit "must" vs "best-effort" behaviors.
- E2E remote scripts validate that common CLI apps render correctly under the chosen TERM profile.

@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+{
+  "name": "save_restore_with_attrs",
+  "description": "ESC 7 / ESC 8 (DECSC/DECRC) saves and restores cursor position and SGR attributes",
+  "initial_size": [10, 5],
+  "input_bytes_hex": "1b5b316d41 1b37 1b5b306d 1b5b333b31484243 1b38 44",
+  "comment": "SGR 1 (bold on), print A at (0,0). ESC 7 saves cursor at (0,1) with bold. SGR 0 resets. CUP(3,1) to (2,0), print BC. ESC 8 restores cursor to (0,1) with bold. Print D at (0,1) with bold.",
+  "expected": {
+    "cursor": { "row": 0, "col": 2 },
+    "cells": [
+      { "row": 0, "col": 0, "char": "A", "attrs": { "bold": true } },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 1, "char": "D", "attrs": { "bold": true } },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 0, "char": "B" },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 1, "char": "C" }
+    ]
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+{
+  "name": "save_restore_dec",
+  "description": "ESC 7 (DECSC) saves cursor, ESC 8 (DECRC) restores it",
+  "initial_size": [10, 5],
+  "input_bytes_hex": "414243 1b37 1b5b333b314844454647 1b38 58",
+  "comment": "Print ABC at (0,0)-(0,2), cursor at (0,3). ESC 7 saves cursor at (0,3). CUP(3,1) to (2,0), print DEFG. ESC 8 restores to (0,3). Print X at (0,3).",
+  "expected": {
+    "cursor": { "row": 0, "col": 4 },
+    "cells": [
+      { "row": 0, "col": 0, "char": "A" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 1, "char": "B" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 2, "char": "C" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 3, "char": "X" },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 0, "char": "D" },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 1, "char": "E" },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 2, "char": "F" },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 3, "char": "G" }
+    ]
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+{
+  "name": "insert_chars_basic",
+  "description": "CSI Ps @ (Insert Characters) shifts existing content right at cursor position",
+  "initial_size": [8, 3],
+  "input_bytes_hex": "41424344451b5b313b32481b5b324058",
+  "comment": "Print ABCDE at (0,0)-(0,4). CUP(1,2) to (0,1). CSI 2 @ inserts 2 blanks at (0,1), shifting BCDE right. Grid becomes A__BCDE (within 8 cols). Print X at (0,1), cursor advances to (0,2).",
+  "expected": {
+    "cursor": { "row": 0, "col": 2 },
+    "cells": [
+      { "row": 0, "col": 0, "char": "A" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 1, "char": "X" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 3, "char": "B" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 4, "char": "C" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 5, "char": "D" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 6, "char": "E" }
+    ]
+  }
+}

+{
+  "name": "delete_lines_in_region",
+  "description": "CSI M (Delete Lines) within a scroll region removes current line and shifts up",
+  "initial_size": [5, 5],
+  "input_bytes_hex": "411b5b323b3148421b5b333b3148431b5b343b3148441b5b353b3148451b5b323b34721b5b323b31481b5b314d58",
+  "comment": "Print A-E on rows 0-4. DECSTBM(2,4) sets scroll region rows 1-3 (0-indexed), cursor -> (0,0). CUP(2,1) to (1,0). DL(1) deletes row 1 (B), C->row1, D->row2, blank->row3. Print X overwrites C at (1,0). Row 0=A, 1=X, 2=D, 3=blank, 4=E.",
+  "expected": {
+    "cursor": { "row": 1, "col": 1 },
+    "cells": [
+      { "row": 0, "col": 0, "char": "A" },
+      { "row": 1, "col": 0, "char": "X" },
+      { "row": 2, "col": 0, "char": "D" },
+      { "row": 4, "col": 0, "char": "E" }
+    ]
+  }
+}

+{

+  "initial_size": [5, 5],
+  "input_bytes_hex": "411b5b323b3148421b5b333b3148431b5b343b3148441b5b353b3148451b5b323b34721b5b323b31481b5b314c58",
+  "comment": "Print A-E on rows 0-4 at col 0. DECSTBM(2,4) sets scroll region rows 1-3 (0-indexed), cursor -> (0,0). CUP(2,1) to (1,0). IL(1) inserts blank at row 1, B->row2, C->row3, D lost. Print X at (1,0). Row 0=A, 1=X, 2=B, 3=C, 4=E.",
+  "expected": {
+    "cursor": { "row": 1, "col": 1 },
+    "cells": [
+      { "row": 0, "col": 0, "char": "A" },

+  "initial_size": [10, 3],
+  "input_bytes_hex": "41c3a942c3bc43",
+  "comment": "Print A (ASCII), then é (U+00E9, 2 bytes: C3 A9), then B (ASCII), then ü (U+00FC, 2 bytes: C3 BC), then C (ASCII). All single-cell width.",
+  "expected": {
+    "cursor": { "row": 0, "col": 5 },
+    "cells": [
+      { "row": 0, "col": 0, "char": "A" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 1, "char": "é" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 2, "char": "B" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 3, "char": "ü" },
+      { "row": 0, "col": 4, "char": "C" }
